name: The Headless Horseman
primary_sources: The Legend of Sleepy Hollow by Washington Irving

visual_attributes.morphology: humanoid figure on horseback, lacking a head
distinctive_features: carries a pumpkin or a spectral head
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: headless rider seated on a horse, often holding a pumpkin or spectral head in hand

domain_specific_functional.narrative_role: antagonist or spectral presence in the story
domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: Halloween icon and symbol of American folklore

relationships.description: The Headless Horseman is perceived as a threat to Ichabod Crane during his ride home.
Brom Bones is suspected of impersonating the Headless Horseman to scare Ichabod Crane.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the blending of American and European folklore, symbolizing fear of the unknown and the supernatural
primary_sources: The Legend of Sleepy Hollow by Washington Irving
